MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Serve David Phillips Electronically

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Continued To Aug-12-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Serve David Phillips Electronically
Off Calendar May-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JOHN ADLER AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion For Leave To Serve David Phillips Electronically.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff John Adler's Motion For Leave To Serve David Phillips Electronically is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff has not demonstrated he timely filed his motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(e) [service by fax only be agreement and adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Plaintiff filed his motion on 5/1/2026, which is not 16 court days before 5/19/2026, the hearing date noticed. Moving Plaintiff's disregard for the rules is not excused.

A party seeking to have a motion heard outside the regular noticed period must secure an order shortening time or advancing the hearing. Petitioner has no such order and, therefore, its attempt to jump the line fails and its untimely motion is ordered off calendar.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare and submit to Dept. 302 a proposed form order consistent with the court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Plaintiff John Adler's unopposed Motion to Admit Pro Hac Vice Counsel Jolene F. LaVigne-Albert is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, counsel Jolene F. LaVigne-Albert may appear as counsel pro hac vice for Moving Party in this action.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
